title: Sectioning of Mouse Proximal Thoracic Aorta
author: Jeff Z. Chen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be9mjh46

steps.title: Euthanize mouse with ketamine
steps.instruction: Euthanize mouse with ketamine : xyalazine cocktail

steps.title: Open thoracic cavity and expose heart
steps.instruction: Open thoracic cavity and expose heart

steps.title: Nick right atrium with sharp scissors
steps.instruction: Nick right atrium with sharp scissors

steps.title: Flush vasculature with cold saline (>5...
steps.instruction: Flush vasculature with cold saline (>5 mL) introduced slowly into the left ventricle

steps.title: Dissect away the thymus, lungs, trachea, and esophagus
steps.instruction: Dissect away the thymus, lungs, trachea, and esophagus

steps.title: Inject optimal cutting temperature compound (OCT; ~0
steps.instruction: Inject optimal cutting temperature compound (OCT; ~0.5 mL) slowly into the left ventricle to keep patent, inject until the lumen of the descending aorta expands

steps.title: Dissect the heart and thoracic aorta...
steps.instruction: Dissect the heart and thoracic aorta by ligating the left/right subclavians and common carotids near the thoracic outlet and ligating the aorta above the diaphragm

steps.title: Dissect the descending aorta from the...
steps.instruction: Dissect the descending aorta from the spine, taking care not to nick the aorta

steps.title: Put a thin layer of OCT in mold
steps.instruction: Put a thin layer of OCT in mold

steps.title: Separate the heart from the aorta...
steps.instruction: Separate the heart from the aorta by holding the heart with the forceps and cutting with either scissors or a scalpel blade, as close to the heart as possible without causing damage. Figure 1: Approximate line to cut ventricle

steps.title: Mount the heart, ascending aorta, and...
steps.instruction: Mount the heart, ascending aorta, and descending aorta with the heart facing the left and descending aorta on the right. With permanent marker, mark where the aorta lays in the mold

steps.title: Cover tissue with OCT
steps.instruction: Cover tissue with OCT. Place in cryostat with peltier on for 00:15:00 for OCT to solidify.

steps.title: Set temperature to -20 °C
steps.instruction: Set temperature to -20 °C

steps.title: Set chuck advance to 10 µm
steps.instruction: Set chuck advance to 10 µm

steps.title: Make sure stage is level
steps.instruction: Make sure stage is level

steps.title: Make sure sectioning starts with new...
steps.instruction: Make sure sectioning starts with new region of blade or new blade

steps.title: Make sure anti roll plate is not chipped
steps.instruction: Make sure anti roll plate is not chipped

steps.title: Label 10 slides / sample / region. For example
steps.instruction: Label 10 slides / sample / region. For example: Study name, mouse ID and group, region collected (ascending or root) and number of slide (1-10)

steps.title: Remove block from mold
steps.instruction: Remove block from mold

steps.title: Trim excess solid OCT from tissue,...
steps.instruction: Trim excess solid OCT from tissue, keeping intact the descending aorta

steps.title: Cut the heart at the mid ventricle level
steps.instruction: Cut the heart at the mid ventricle level

steps.title: Put small drop of OCT onto mount
steps.instruction: Put small drop of OCT onto mount

steps.title: Mount trimmed tissue block with the...
steps.instruction: Mount trimmed tissue block with the aortic arch facing up onto holder

steps.title: Place in peltier and wait 10...
steps.instruction: Place in peltier and wait 10 minutes for interface to solidify

steps.title: The method requires cutting sequential sections...
steps.instruction: The method requires cutting sequential sections in the correct orientation and without tearing, curling, or sticking incorrectly to the slide. Therefore, do not cut aortas collected from your experiments until you are sure you can reliably cut sections sequentially Place holder onto cryostat chuck with the heart facing down and the descending aorta on top.

steps.title: Start sectioning, checking every 10 cycles...
steps.instruction: Start sectioning, checking every 10 cycles until tissue can be seen.

steps.title: Start collecting serial sections when arch...
steps.instruction: Start collecting serial sections when arch divides into ascending aorta and descending aorta. Ascending aorta should be towards you and descending aorta should be away. This should produce sections with both the ascending aorta and descending aorta. Ascending aortas should be towards the top of the slide, descending aortas should be towards the bottom. Verify orientation of sections by counting the number of elastic laminae seen: Ascending = 8-10 lamina, Descending = 8-6 lamina.

steps.title: Collect at least 9 serial sections per slide
steps.instruction: Collect at least 9 serial sections per slide. (Fig 2) Figure 2: The organization of sections on consecutive slides Collect first section produced on bottom left corner of slide 1. Collect second section on bottom left corner of slide 2 and subsequent section in this mode. Collect eleventh section to the right of first section on slide 1. Collect serial sections by holding labeled slide downward with the label facing you. The ascending aorta should be on towards the top of the slide and the descending on bottom of slide

steps.title: If section rolls or flips -...
steps.instruction: If section rolls or flips - use antistatic brush to unroll section

steps.title: Stop collecting at the level of...
steps.instruction: Stop collecting at the level of the aortic root - where cusps of valve leaflets are seen. Serial sections are collected at 10 μm/section, and 8 to 10 slides/aorta until the aortic wall disappears or is not intact anymore. Usually at least 9 serial sections/slide can be collected.

steps.title: Lock chuck wheel
steps.instruction: Lock chuck wheel

steps.title: Remove block from chuck and mount...
steps.instruction: Remove block from chuck and mount - save or discard excess tissue

steps.title: Brush clean all surfaces with 70% ethanol and brush
steps.instruction: Brush clean all surfaces with 70% ethanol and brush

steps.title: Discard trimmings
steps.instruction: Discard trimmings

steps.title: Turn off light
steps.instruction: Turn off light

steps.title: Close cryostat window
steps.instruction: Close cryostat window

steps.title: Slides should be stored in a...
steps.instruction: Slides should be stored in a appropriately labeled slide box (both the top and one of the short edges of the box) at -20̊C.

steps.title: Cut away approximately 60 - 70%...
steps.instruction: Cut away approximately 60 - 70% of the ventricles (the red line shown in the Fig 1 )
